coverage_notes: OpenReview api2 (Cloudflare Turnstile challenge solved via headed Chrome) covers: ICLR 2024/2025/2026, NeurIPS 2023/2024/2025, ICML 2023/2024/2025 — filtered by content.venue containing 'oral' (case-insensitive). OpenReview api1 (v1, no challenge) covers ICLR 2021/2022 and NeurIPS 2021 via the note 'venue' field ('... Oral'). ICLR 2023: OpenReview abolished oral/spotlight naming; used 'notable top 5%' (90 papers, the oral-equivalent top tier, mapped here to award='oral') and 'notable top 25%' (spotlight-equivalent). NeurIPS 2022: NO separate oral tier exists — OpenReview decisions are only 'Accept'; neurips.cc virtual site (neurips-2022-orals-posters.json) has only eventtype Poster/Spotlight and the /virtual/2022/events/oral page is empty. 0 orals, not a data gap. ICML 2021/2022: not on OpenReview; taken from icml.cc virtual site JSON (icml-YYYY-orals-posters.json) eventtype=='Oral' (the long-talk/oral tier), authors=fullname list, source_url=PMLR proceedings URL. ICML 2026: skipped (conference in progress). No entries were fabricated; every entry traces to an OpenReview forum id or an icml.cc virtual record.
